CATaN 0.1.0

    • CATaN: Canonical correlation Analysis of Transcriptome and TF-gene regulatory Networks

Filter low-expression genes from a count matrix

Source: R/filter_low_expression.R
filter_low_expression.Rd

Removes genes that are not expressed above specified thresholds in a sufficient proportion of samples. Applies two filters: one on raw counts and one on CPM values. Only genes passing both filters are retained.

filter_low_expression(
  counts,
  min_count = 10,
  min_cpm = 1,
  min_prop = 0.15,
  verbose = TRUE
)

Arguments

counts

A numeric matrix of raw counts (genes x samples) or a SummarizedExperiment object.

min_count

Numeric. Minimum raw count threshold (default 10).

min_cpm

Numeric. Minimum CPM threshold (default 1).

min_prop

Numeric. Minimum proportion of samples that must exceed both thresholds (default 0.15).

verbose

Logical. Print filtering summary (default TRUE).

Value

A numeric matrix of filtered counts (genes x samples).

Details

This function replicates the filtering strategy used in the CATaN pipeline:

  1. Compute CPM using edgeR::cpm().

  2. Identify genes where at least min_prop of samples have raw count >= min_count.

  3. Identify genes where at least min_prop of samples have CPM >= min_cpm.

  4. Retain genes passing both criteria.

Examples

set.seed(1)
counts <- matrix(rnbinom(1000, mu = 10, size = 1), nrow = 100, ncol = 10)
rownames(counts) <- paste0("gene", seq_len(100))
filtered <- filter_low_expression(counts)
#> Low expression filtering: 100 -> 96 genes (removed 4)
#>   Criteria: count >= 10 and CPM >= 1 in >= 15% samples
